src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.why_exists: Java 21 enforces stricter serialization checks; without @Serial, the compiler may flag or reject serialVersionUID fields not annotated as serializable members.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.why_exists: Java 21 enforces stricter serialization checks; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.general_idea: Import java.io.Serial to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/servic.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is in java.io package; without the import, the code will not compile.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without the import, the code will not compile.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: Java 14+ introduces the @Serial annotation for serialization-related members; while not strictly a compile error in all configurations, it is a required migration step for strict Java 21 compliance and modern serialization practices to avoid warnings or errors in strict mode.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without this import, the code will fail to compile.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial on serialVersionUID, the code may fail to compile with --enable-preview or strict linting rules enabled in modern toolchains.

src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/com/daangcool/stack/web/re.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compilation error.
